name: Cacciucco (Weir)
recipeIngredient: crushed red pepper flakes - a large pinch
2 cups fish stock (or bottled clam juice)
3 pounds white fish such as monkfish, cod, halibut, bass or sole
1 pound fresh shrimp, cleaned and cut into rings
recipeInstructions:
1. Warm the olive oil in a large soup pot over medium heat.
2. Add the onions and cook, stirring occasionally, until soft, about 10 minutes.
3. Add the sliced garlic and red pepper flakes; continue to cook, stirring, for 2 minutes.
4. Add the tomatoes, parsley, wine, fish stock (or clam juice), and water. Reduce the heat to low and simmer until the liquid has thickened slightly, 20 to 30 minutes.
5. Add the fish, clams, and shrimp to the pan and simmer until the fish is cooked, about 10 minutes.
6. While the fish is cooking, toast the bread. Rub both sides of each toast with whole garlic cloves.
7. Add the calamari to the pot and simmer until it is white and cooked through, 1 minute. Season with salt and pepper.
8. To serve, place a slice of toasted bread on the bottom of each serving bowl and spoon the soup over the toast. Serves 8.
[PER SERVING: 465 calories, 61 g protein, 24 g carbohydrate, 12 g fat (2 g saturated), 241 mg cholesterol, 415 mg sodium, 1 g fiber.]
